compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Workflow-vs-bespoke parity nit found in tepid-bus-pomp review: bespoke `pass4_coach` returns `[]` with
NO LLM call when there are 0 surviving advisories; the workflow ALWAYS runs the `coach_notes` prompt
step, so a clean PASS (0 advisories) still makes one wasted coach LLM call (and a coach listing over an
empty set). Pre-dates B5 — the coach step has always run since B2. Gate it to match bespoke.

## What

- Gate the workflow `coach_notes` prompt step on `surviving > 0` (a `when:` condition) so a PASS with 0
  surviving advisories makes no coach LLM call.
- Keep the `coach_inputs` / `coach` ops behaving correctly (empty coaching list) when skipped.

## Scope

In scope: the `when:` gate on the `coach_notes` step in
`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` (both the agentic and single-turn branches) and a test
asserting the coach call count is 0 on a 0-surviving PASS. Out of scope: WS1 migration, WS2 model/editor,
WS3 chunking. This story is independent of the others and can land in parallel.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The workflow `coach_notes` step is gated on `surviving > 0` (in both the agentic and single-turn
      branches).
- [ ] A PASS with 0 surviving advisories makes no coach LLM call; a test asserts the call count is 0.
- [ ] A PASS/BLOCK with ≥1 surviving advisory still runs the coach step (no regression).
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: WS4: Gate workflow coach_notes on surviving>0 (bespoke parity, no coach call on clean PASS)
